core_model.structure_summary: A first-order stochastic differential equation for a single scalar driven by additive white noise, with a drift that combines a linear dissipative term and a discontinuous sign-of-state term, optionally with a constant forcing.

variables.meaning: scalar dynamical variable whose relaxation and correlations are sought
additive delta-correlated white noise driving the state
amplitude of the state-independent sign(x) drift (piecewise-constant restoring term)

notes: Two candidate cores: the underlying Langevin SDE (drift = sign term + linear damping + additive white noise) versus its associated Fokker-Planck PDE whose time-dependent propagator and correlation functions are the actual results. I take the SDE as the core model per SDE<->Fokker-Planck duality; the discontinuous sign(x) drift produces matching conditions at x=0 (u(0-)=u(0+) and a jump in derivative), so boundary/matching conditions are structurally essential. Extracted equations are mostly solution formulas (parabolic-cylinder-function series) rather than the defining equation.
